name: Marions Best Ever Apple Pie
recipeIngredient: 3 ounces each of cold butter and cold lard
5 large tart apples (such as Granny Smith)
recipeInstructions:
To make pastry, cut butter and lard into flour using a pastry blender until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Scatter ice-cold water over the mixture, mixing with a fork until dough forms a ball. Wrap in plastic wrap and chill for about 30 minutes. Divide into two balls. Roll out one portion to fit a 9-inch pie pan, leaving 1 inch of pastry at the edge. Preheat oven to 425°F.
Core, peel, and thinly slice apples. Toss with cinnamon, sugar, and lemon juice in a bowl. Sprinkle heaping tablespoon of flour or bread crumbs evenly over the pie shell. Arrange apple slices on top, leaving as little space between slices as possible. Pile slices high but do not mound in the center. Roll out other half of dough to fit the top of the pie. Brush the edge of the bottom crust with egg wash made by blending beaten egg yolk with milk. Place top layer of dough over the pie and tuck it under the bottom layer of dough around the edge of the pie. Crimp edges with fingers to form fluting. Prick the pie in at least a dozen places with a fork to allow steam to escape. Brush with egg wash. Bake for 15 minutes. Reduce oven temperature to 400°F and bake for another 15 minutes, then reduce to 325°F and bake for an additional 20 minutes, or until crust is golden brown.
